title: Gymnosphaera biformis
taxonomy: Gymnosphaera biformis is classified as a species within the genus Gymnosphaera, which belongs to the family Gymnosphaeraceae. This plant is a type of green algae. The genus Gymnosphaera is part of the class Chlorophyceae. Chlorophyceae is a class of green algae that belongs to the phylum Chlorophyta. Gymnosphaera biformis is also known for its distinctive biconvex cell shape.

intro: Gymnosphaera biformis, also known as the 'two-horned' plant, is a species of fern that belongs to the family Blechnaceae. Traditionally, this plant has been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The bioactive constituents of Gymnosphaera biformis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenes, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
